abstract: Automated SPC analysis of 'manufacturing_process_dataset' (300 observations, 7 variables) by SigmaFlow v10. 1 statistical anomaly(ies) detected (1 critical, 0 warnings). Regression R²=0.084. 1 column(s) flagged as non-normal.

insights.description: Rule 1: 2 point(s) outside the 3σ control limits (at indices [128, 174]).
insights.meaning: A data point beyond ±3σ from the mean is statistically rare (~0.27% chance under normality). Its presence strongly suggests a special-cause event — something changed in the process at that moment.
insights.recommendation: Identify and investigate the specific points flagged. Check for equipment malfunction, operator changes, raw material variation, or measurement errors. Remove confirmed special causes before computing capability indices.

insights.description: No statistically significant trend detected in the process data.
insights.meaning: The Spearman correlation coefficient (τ = -0.018) and p-value (p = 0.7629) indicate no systematic drift in the process over time.
insights.recommendation: Continue routine monitoring. The process is temporally stable.
